After about a couple of weeks using it, I discovered all kinds of undesirable things coming out of the pores of my skin, I developed little pussy sores and pimples, but the pimples were not like the regular pimple, these were deep and what came out was really think and sometimes hard. Instead of having what I thought was an allergic reaction, I think this product was pulling impurities out of my skin and detoxing it. I also had a couple of small, sebaceous cysts on my forehead and after about a month of using this product, they were gone! I was told by a plastic surgeon the only way to get rid of them was surgery but I didn't want the scar on my forehead so never had anything done about them, I had them for years. To this day, the cysts are gone. My skin no longer reacts as if impurities are being pulled out. There is still a small amount of burning on the nights I use this product but if I follow it up with argon oil or rose hip oil, the burning goes away. I've noticed my skin is a firmer, smoother in texture, very soft, and the skin tone is more even. Although I got amazing results, this product may be overpriced considering it is only ascorbic acid. Maybe the products in the activator justifies the price but as I'm not an expert on ingredients, I'm not sure.

product_title: Ikon B1 Slant Head Razor Head
clean_review_text: I have about 10 razors. I have been in search of better and better shaves. My Feather AS-D2 is awesome and smooth but perhaps a bit too mild. I fine even the Merkur 34C a little too aggressive for my skin. The Merkur Futur is horrible for me and is a paperweight for anyone who prefers mild razors. The iKon B1 slant is a perfect balance. Not too aggressive, and with a light touch it gave me a very smooth shave. I normally do 3 passes and then touch up areas that need it. I had 5 days growth when I shaved with this razor head today. I was amazed when I finished my first pass. It looked like I had done two passes with my other razors. I normally do with the grain, cross-grain, and then lightly against the grain. With the B1 I did a with-the-grain pass and one very light across-the-grain pass and it looked like I was done shaving. I did a touch-up in one or two spots and I called it good. An against the grain pass would have left me absolutely BBS but that usually gives me ingrown hairs. Oh, by the way, no nicks, weepers, or irritation at all. The head is shaped very weird with the slant design but I was able to shave under my nostrils with ease; much better than most, actually! I really am impressed with this head. I can't wait to do another shave tomorrow. My shave: iKon B1 head iKon OSS handle from my 101 Persona Lab Blue blade (2nd shave on the blade) Stirling Soap Co. British Leather shaving soap Stirling Soap Co. Coniferous Pre-Shave oil

clean_review_text: After trying several of the 5 magnet type, I found these and I'm so glad I did! The 5 magnet type never worked after I tried to cut them to the right size because there was never a magnet on the end, which made it impossible to get them to stay on without using glue. I used these for my wedding and they stayed on all day/evening and weren't too heavy feeling like some lashes. They really made my eyes pop and stand out beautifully in pictures! My skin is more radiant and soft and the expression lines in my forehead appear a little softer and the improved clarity of my skin is noticeable. clean_review_text: The good the bad and the ugly. Here goes. I've always sworn by Norelco trimmers. I've owned several. They normally last reasonably well. I normally get 5 years of use out of them. I say reasonably well because in this day and age, 5 years is good but I prefer things to last 20 years like they used too. Sadly; that's not going to happen. The adjustable guide is the best design that I have found. Far better than similar units that use multiple guides that can get lost, damaged and require additional storage space. The turbo function does work, it has enough high power that calling it "turbo" is not only cool but accurate. The charge/low battery lights function well. The vacuum functions well. I've tried other manufactures so called vacuum units and found that not only did they not work they were a total disappointment. This vacuum system works! Removing and installing the guide was a bit tricky at first but you get used to it. The single thing that I miss is the slide up mini trimmer. Frankly; I would rather have that than the vacuum. product_title: stila Perfect Me, Perfect Hue Eye & Cheek Palette, Fair/Light
clean_review_text: I purchased this product in a store, but I am such a fan I had to write a review. Ever since I purchased this palette, it has been my go-to for everyday makeup. I am fair-skinned with dark hair and hazel eyes, and the colors are perfect. I don't always use every color in a palette and I don't always place the colors as directed, but in this case, Stila knows what they are talking about! The large color on the left is your all-over base, and I apply that first. Next, I go to the top right corner and apply this grayish color from crease to browbone. Third, I take the darkest color and place it quite heavily (but narrowly) in the crease and a little in the corner and lash line. Next, I put the highlight (lightest) color just under the brow and also outside the inner corner of the eye. Finally, I put the shimmery gold-like color in the middle across the lid and blend all the colors with a blending brush. I use the darker blush normally, but use the light one as a highlight if I want the added effect. I love that when I am doing my makeup on the run, I only have to grab this one container and my makeup brushes (I have the EcoTools Eye Enhancing Duo Brush set with four tips on two brushes, so even there you don't have to take as much with you). The quality of the product is excellent and it blends well. Other than the darkest brown, the eyeshadow pigments are not super intense, so you can go light or apply more for a deeper intensity.

product_title: DROP THE BOMB Bbosong Sun Stick SPF50+ PA++++ 15g - Oil Free UVA/UVB Protection Sebum Control Skin Smooth Moisturizing Sunblock Stick Balm without Sticky, Primer before Makeup
clean_review_title: excellent sunscreen with skin loving ingredients, broad spectrum, great little container
clean_review_text: The "Drop the Bomb" BBOSONG Suns stick uses one of THEE best UVA/UVB sunscreen ingredients on the planet: "Bis-Ethylhexyloxyphenol Methoxyphenyl Triazine"! Say that 5 times fast. Or once. lol. It is a broad spectrum, highly photo stable, chemical sunscreen. Chemical sunscreens mean that they absorb rays and disperse as heat before they can reach skin and do damage. The SPF rating of 50+ and PA++++ means that it offers the highest amount of protection that a sunscreen can offer. Korean skin care, particularly sunscreens, can use super effective ingredients that products made in (or for) USA can not have because the governing bodies have not approved it. It is not because they are unsafe, but because there is a significant amount of bureaucracy involved. No new sunscreen ingredients have been approved in the USA in over 15 years, sad since there are so many wonderful innovations in the rest of the world. We lag greatly behind. Fortunately, we can still buy these products. The other sunscreen used in this product is, Ethylhexyl Methoxycinnamate (Octinoxate)- a less stellar chemical sunscreen that has mixed thoughts on its efficacy, safety, and desirability. It protects only against UVB rays and breaks down when exposed to light (10% per 30-40 minutes). However, when combined with another broad spectrum sunscreen, it compliments it very well. The third sunscreen component is, Octocrylene. Screens out the UVB and somewhat in the UVA II range. It is quite photostable (loses 10% of SPF protection in 95 mins) and is often used to stabilize other photo-unstable UV-filters (octinoxate) It is also often used to improve the water resistance of the products. As for all the other ingredients- primarily stabilizers, viscosity controlling ingredients, hardeners-- basically all ingredients that make it a STICK than can glide onto the skin. It is formulated to melt into the skin on contact, and yet absorb thoroughly. Basically, you won't even know it is there. And like so many other Korean skin care items, there are ingredients that go above and beyond the basic sunscreen-- included are skin loving ingredients (emollients, skin conditioners, soothers) such as Japanese knotweed, water lily, gotu kola (centella asiatica), camellia japonica, Lespedeza Capitata Extract (anti inflammatory), scutellaria (ie scullcap/mint), green tea, licorice, rosemary, chamomile Has a super mild scent- clean, fresh almost like a deodorant, but subtle. Happens to be a in a container that reminds me of deodorant. Super small, cute. Stick in a pocket or purse. I happen to love using it on my hands. I am in my 40s, and while I have been using SPF most of my life on my face, I figured it was high time to remember to put some on my hands. I like keeping this in my purse or my car, and applying it when I go to drive. That seems to be the time that my hands are most consistently exposed to the sun. Figure, hands on the steering wheel and in front of a big window. So yeh, this is perfect for that. I have no problem using this on my face. Doesn't grab skin when used over a moisturizer. If need be can be applied/emulsified in palms then patted on. I prefer it as my "reapply" SPF rather than my first application product (I am used to a cream style). product_title: Benton Aloe Propolis Soothing Gel & Benton Cleansing Foam with 2 Benton Masks ( Bundle )
clean_review_text: This kit is a great deal! I've been a devoted user of Korean skin care products. For a while, I have preferred, out of several brands and types of sheet mask, the Benton Bee and Snail sheet mask. Fantastic product!!!! Firms, smooths, soothes. It's great. However, that's not why I purchased this set. I have combo skin that leans towards oily in the heat. Super oily T-zone. After doing a ton of research, I decided to give the Benton Aloe Gel moisturizer a try. I'd also heard great things about this foam cleanser. I'm really enjoying the cleanser so far. I wet my hands and face, use only a pea sized amount, rub my hands together to create the foam (not a soapy lather, but a thick and creamy foam), and work over my face. I do this after oil cleansing. This does a great job of picking up any oil residue and dirt, while leaving my face soft and refreshed. Never tight and dry, which is the sign of an awful cleanser. The gel moisturizer is okay. Just okay. I don't get as oily as I did with lotion moisturizers, which is great. However, I don't have that supple, smooth feel that I'm used to and prefer. I've actually noticed flaking around my nose, which is something I never dealt with after moisturizing. A pea sized amount covers my entire face, it leaves a slightly tacky finish until absorbed ( I always let each of my products absorb for around 90 seconds before applying the next step), and is completely colorless. Will I repurchase? No, but I do suggest that you give this a try if your research and needs point you this direction. Everyone's skin care needs can vary on several levels. There are universal musts for good skin health, like cleansing daily, moisturizing, and using a good sunscreen. There a a ton a variations of those products, though. Finding the right routine and products can be a trial and error process. The main thing is to stick to it. The results are worth it!

product_title: Precision Clean Oral-B Vitality Rechargeable Electric Toothbrush
clean_review_title: Much better then the Sonic Spinbrush by Arm & Hammer
clean_review_text: Until a year ago, I never liked the idea of owning an electric toothbrush: I'd owned several cheap ones and never liked any of them. Then I figured that I'd try one again about a year ago and so I chose the Sonic Spinbrush by Arm & Hammer. I chose the Spinbrush because it had a large brush, which I thought meant less time/effort brushing my teeth. The Spinbrush worked pretty well, but the large brush head gagged me horribly. Because of this, I decided to give the Oral-B Vitality a try and I like it much better. The first reason I prefer the Vitality is because it has a much smaller brush head (you don't need a large brush with an electric toothbrush, since the toothbrush is doing all the work.) The smaller brush head also allows me to reach places the larger Spinbrush cannot reach, because it's too big. The Vitality is easier to maneuver in your hand as well, thanks to the smaller handle base. Another thing I didn't like about the Spinbrush is how it would click off automatically after 2 minutes (I don't think all models of the Spinbrush do this, though.) The Vitality purposely sputters after 2 minutes for it's timing function, but then continues it's normal operation after a second or two. I still find this annoying, but at least I don't have to remove it from my mouth, dripping with toothpaste, just to turn it back on. And if I finish early, the Spinbrush required me to punch the button twice to stop it (again, something not easy to do with a vibrating toothbrush in your mouth), whereas the Vitality only requires a single button push. These two things alone make the Vitality much more convenient. I also think the Vitality is more powerful then the Spinbrush. The reason you had to push the button on the Spinbrush twice was so you could use it at a lower speed if necessary, but I never found that necessary at all. I prefer the more powerful Vitality, because it feels like it's cleaning better by not slowing down if I push too hard. About the only thing I liked better about the Spinbrush was that it had an indicator light on the base to tell you it was receiving power (I think it gauged the charge, too, but that was never a problem for me.) I'm not sure if that's even important, but it is something I tend to prefer with rechargeable items. Other than that, I love the Vitality and I much prefer it over the Spinbrush. Now I can brush my teeth without gagging and I am thankful for that.

I use it with the CND CuticleAway Cuticle Remover gel, and it works just as expected. The nail cleaner end is a nice touch - I prefer this style to the ones that have flat or split trimmer blade ends. One caveat - the edge on the pusher end is sharp - you need to be careful of the pressure and angle used which pushing your cuticles back, as holding the tool at the wrong angle and/or using too much pressure may result in damage to the top layer of your nail (e.g. the tool may shave a slight bit off). I used my Dremel with a fine sanding bit to dull the edge on the pusher end; this resolved the issue for me. If you don't have a Dremel, I suggest taking an emery board to smooth the sharp end before use in order to avoid damaging your nail. product_title: Hatteker Men's Electric Shaver Foil Shaver Wet Dry Electric Razor Rechargeable with Pop Up Precision Trimmer Replacenment Blade Cordless
clean_review_text: Just had my first shave with this. I've used two electric razors in my life. One was an already ancient one that my dad had, about 40 years ago. Ripped each of the few hairs in my face out one by one. Then an early Norelco 3-head circular job, about 30 years ago that either sucked or I didn't know how to use. Very even stubble was the result of that. Hence my refusal to try one until my memories have faded. This review reflects one shave - not a long term review. I'm impressed. It's slower than I expected, I half-thought I could activate an app on my phone or something and I would be shaved (I'm joking!). It's pretty darn close to a manual 5-blade fresh razor. Here are the conditions under which I'm reporting: - I'm 62, so my skin isn't exactly taut on my face. - Not a heavy beard, I need to shave every day now, but not until I was about 35. - This shave was with 2 days of stubble. - I usually shave in the shower, using Braille to tell when a patch is smooth. I used the razor dry. The manual said to charge the razor before first use, but it came out of the box showing 100% charge so I went for it. The manual also says not to apply too much pressure, so I was tentative at first. It helps to apply fair pressure while moving the razor SLOWLY through that fungus that seems to grow constantly on our faces. Stretching my skin with other-handed fingers in most spots helped. I used a mirror just to watch the progress, but you can use your sonic sensors, AKA ears, to tell when the blades are and aren't removing stubble. I don't think a mirror is at all necessary, YMMV. While I've been writing this, I've used Braille again to check on the usual trouble spots - I can just grab the mower and run it over the rough bits while I'm doing this. You could do this easily while driving somewhere - no risk of a small knick, and the aural attention that it takes doesn't really distract from driving. I didn't notice when I ordered this razor that it came with an extra foil. Nice detail, since the foil is easy to damage being thin (as it should be). The kit comes with a carrying bag, which is nice to keep everything together while traveling. I would find a small rigid container for the extra foil if I were to travel with that - it could easily get bent in the carrying bag knocking around with all the bits. The foil is too long for the old canisters from film cameras, but a cut-off from a roll of Xmas paper would suffice. The grip is comfortable and makes the shaving head easy to maneuver. The carrying bag contains everything in a small package - I'm a Cardinal (Archbishops and above don't shave) in the Church of Small Luggage and I will happily carry this instead of a couple of manual razors on any trip. Finally (applause erupts from the crowd), very nearly close shave as a manual razor, a little slower than a manual razor, works very well dry (absolutely unlike a manual razor, though I've tried that also and it does sort of work). Buy it. Try it.

product_title: Catrice | SUNGASM Luminizing Bronzer 01 | For A Sun-Kissed Shimmery Finish | Highly Pigmented & Blendable | Vegan, Paraben Free, Oil Free | Cruelty Free
clean_review_title: GReat Bronzer For Medium To Lighter Skin Tones, But Great Eye Shadow Too- Read On
clean_review_text: As a makeup artist, I am always trying to find ways to get the most mileage out of the products that I use. This bronzer had some surprises to it. Read on.. PHOTO- My photo has swatches of both color 1 and 2 which are labeled. I tried to get accurate photos colorwise and it is hard to do as the camera does not capture the nuances. When applied with a brush, the effect is MUCH lighter. WHAT IS THIS LIKE? COMPACT The compact is a high layered cardboard with a strong hinge to it. There is no mirror and the pan inside has the product with designs pressed into it. It does not come with a brush. WHAT IS THE COLOR LIKE? This color is more of a bronze gold color. It is still on the cooler side, but light warmer skin tones could use this too. TYPE OF SKIN TESTED ON? This was tested on both MAC translation of skin tones NW 25 (which is what you see in the photos) and NC37 which is a more tan but cooler skin tone. HOW TO APPLY? This applies well but you have to be sure you find the right brush. A stippling brush was the best way to get a natural look from this bronzer and highlighter. It allows for subtle application so that it does not look like you have on too much. If you use too dense of a brush, it is easy to get a stripe of glow on your face that others will see and it can really look odd. You also need to get a fresh brush and blend it very well into your skin. Apply this in the backwards 3 figure on each side of your face. Imagine two 3s in a mirror image on your face with the tips and centers touching. ANY OTHER USES? I found that this also makes a fabulous eye shadow. I found it to be a perfect match with the other color in this series of number 2 which is a darker more rose brown color. This is a great companion to that. If this is all you have, It also makes a great eye shadow alone or with another lighter satin finish shadow or even a matte. I would not go super frosty. HOW TO APPLY TO EYES In terms of eyes, you can use this as a contour, all over color, or in combination with the other darker color of this Sungasm series or another set of shadows. The application is best for a lighter look to use a teardrop shaped brush. Then for a heavier look with more color, a flat eye shadow brush packing this gently into place over a good eye primer is the way to go. For a quick application, you can also use a finger to apply and blend it with a brush or your finger ANY IRRITATION PROBLEMS? I am VERY sensitive and did not have any problems with this product on the face or eyes. THE BOTTOM LINE As long as you are willing to take some time to find the right brush for the application of this, this bronzer is a pretty color especially for cooler toned skin. It is a good highlighter for darker skin tones as a highlighter, though. This is beautiful on the face, eyes, and in tandem with the other color of this bronzer too. MY RATING FIVE STARS. This is a nice bronzer and I am surprised at how well it works on cooler skin tones, something that is not all that easy to find, especially in TWO colors of a collections.

clean_review_title: Quite happy with this! A great find.
clean_review_text: I had some reservations... I’ve seen the round wood ones around, but never made the leap. I was afraid the ceramic would make this too heavy and bulky, but it does not. It’s quite lightweight and because it’s vertical, saves some space. It comes with extensive directions - a nice change - but is really very easy to use and set up. It’s very hot and humid where I live, so I was also worried that adding more humidity to the air would be a bad thing, however, the air that comes out is quite cool, which seems to cool down the area around it (and by “it”, I mean me). I found it very comfortable. I wasn’t sure how I felt about the colored lights option, but I love it! You can choose red, or a brighter red, and also green and a soft lavender blue with their corresponding brighter shades. You can choose to have your color, or opt to have the unit cycle through them all. It’s quite restful. You can, of course, just use the white light. You can set the steam for a continuous output or intermittent. The until must be cleaned after 10 hours of use. It doesn’t hold a great deal of water, so I was wondering if that was going to be inconvenient. I ran it for two continuous hours and I could tell that filling the tank twice would be about 10 hours. So THAT’S easy to keep track of! All in all, I couldn’t be happier with this unit! It checks all the boxes and I can enjoy my essential oils in an peaceful, comforting environment. Highly recommended!!

product_title: ZooArts Portable Electric Ionic Hairbrush Takeout Mini Hair Brush Comb Massager Small (Blue)
clean_review_title: works well, but has a minor design flaw
clean_review_text: I have used ionizing brushes for over 30 years—since they were first sold by Sharper Image. The negative ions work very well to flatten the surface of the cuticle of each hair, making the hair less likely to break or split, and also softer. This particular brush works well, but the one I received has a minor design flaw: the brush part pops off so easily that I have to hold it tight with my thumb, making it less ergonomic than it might be... not an issue for me. As for the vibrating part, I couldn't tell, but that's not what I bought it for. This brush generates NO heat, which is exactly what I wanted. Unlike some similar products, there are NO seams that could catch the hair. TIPS FOR ALL IONIZING BRUSHES: (1) if they don't need electricity (cord or batteries), they're not the real deal (2) heating elements, just like heated blow-drying, tend to dry out your hair and make it more brittle: even oily hair can get dried out (3) brush your hair SLOWLY to get the best result (4) if your hair is flatter after using it, and you don't like that, bend over and brush it upside down to get more volume

clean_review_title: Compression in all the right places; helps with my plantar fasciitis; runs large
clean_review_text: I have plantar fasciitis and have been trying and using various compression socks and sleeves and arch inserts to help reduce the pain and prevent a recurrence once the pain is gone. These socks are not only stylish, but they feel great on and actually have compression in the arch area. There is also good compression in the calf area. I ordered the large/extra-large because I take a 9 to 9.5 shoe. However, I find that these are a bit long (and looser than their small/medium) as I have to roll the top over to prevent it from coming above my knee. They fit me perfectly, otherwise. However, since they are on the large side, I'm passing them off to my boyfriend. I have several pairs of these socks and have worn their socks all day. It feels great wearing them. I also love the styles they come in. These are not only "fun" but they are medically helpful for my plantar fasciitis. I wash socks like these in a lingerie bag and lay them flat to dry. Currently, there is no price listed on Amazon for these socks but I would expect them to be priced in the $12.99 to $16.99 range. The only reason I lowered my rating one star is because they are larger than the size specified; otherwise, these are 5-star socks in my opinion.

product_title: CCW Tactical Holster Shirt for Concealed Carry Compression Fit Clothing with Right and Left Hand Draw Handgun and Magazine Pockets, All Season Moisture Wicking, Mens, Black, XL
clean_review_text: Shirt material is comfortable. form fitting, and tight. Pistol pockets are big enough to hold your full size automatic or 3-4" revolver and will keep it tight against your body. With practice it can be retrieved almost as fast as a shoulder holster. Equally quick access, the same access point has a place for your magazine if you're wearing a lite jacket or vest that won't imprint, otherwise the magazine will show. Design accommodates left and right handed shooters, or both if you can manage two firearms at once. This is actually a brilliant concept for the right body type as pictured. Pockets are big enough for most firearms and will hold them tight against your body. Otherwise unless your wearing a heavy jacket the firearm will imprint and be as obvious as a shoulder holster. If your a heavy sweater this isn't a good choice as the firearm will be wet from your body and/or armpit sweat. If your of the body type pictured and need to carry deep cover wearing a suit without having to worry if your firearm will be seen with an open suit jacket, this is for you. A vest would add concealment and be helpful allowing you to remove your jacket and still conceal your firearm. This would also be a good choice for carrying your backup, again for the right body type. Well crafted and quality materials make the investment worthy. YMMV.

clean_review_text: These are very soft and thin. To me, the fabric is also a little bit warm, so unless you're always cold or going for the Saran wrap sweaty weight loss feel, these might only work for the cooler months. For reference, I'm 4'11", have a 34" waist, and a 42" hip measurement. I ordered these in size large, and they fit, but they are looser than I'd like. The waistband is too big and comes all the way up to my bra, there's too much fabric in the front, and they are several inches too long. There is no elastic in the waistband and also no tummy control whatsoever. The fabric is extremely stretchy, almost too stretchy, especially in the waist area. On me, they are more like maternity pants than regular pants. Being a seamstress, I will end up taking the waistband off and making it much shorter and tighter, and will add a 2-inch elastic insert for a more comfortable and stable fit. I tried folding the waistband down, but it just ends up in a wadded mess. I will also take off the extra length and hem them to fit my short legs. Even with the things I mentioned, the pants are very soft and extremely comfortable, I would just order a size small next time. They are going to be great for wearing around the house during the fall and winter.

I like how it swivels, too, so it never twists around the wrong way. The inside pocket is protected by a zipper and by four outside snaps. This means that nothing is ever going to fall out if you don't want it to. And, inside the inside pocket, it's divided into three different compartments, one of which has an entirely separate zipper. Then, there's another small zip pocket, two pen pockets, and two small pockets. On the outside, there's another zip pocket. Basically, there are tons of ways you can organize anything you want to put in this bag. The zippers are very high quality, which I always look for in a bag. Most of the zippers have leather handles. It's big enough to fit a small or medium laptop, but a giant laptop probably won't fit. And, the size is great. It looks small on the outside, but it holds a lot of stuff. Pros: +High quality zippers +About ten pockets and compartments +Feels very nice and comfortable +Looks extremely attractive Cons: -The buckles are just for decoration. clean_review_title: Would be 5 stars if it had nose wire
clean_review_text: There are 2 masks for under $14 at the time of this review which means they are reasonably priced. The fabric is synthetic but water resistant which I prefer to cotton masks which are more comfortable but water absorbent. I feel the water resistance is better for keeping out viruses. I put some drops of water on the mask and it beaded and after giving it a good hand wash it still beaded so water resistance is good. As described it does indeed appear to have 3 layers. The liner is a knit, again synthetic but it's not bad. Sewing is good. Included elastic bands are around 12 inches so pretty generous and you can tie to fit. My only con is there isn't a nose wire to make it seal tighter to the face but it is understandable given it's machine washable and a wire would probably squish in a washing machine. My head is on the small side and it fits me fine so thinking if you have a large head this might be on the small side but there are pictures uploaded with measuring tape so you can figure out of this will fit you. All masks do get warm and this one is no different but I found it tolerable. In the pictures it said the inner lining was antibacterial. I have no way to test this but antibacterial will not help again virus as it's not a bacteria but it will help protect against other things. I am depending more on the water resistance to help keep out virus contamination. I also don't imagine the antibacterial quality will last forever. Overall good mask and good value.

product_title: Sorcery and Religion in Ancient Scandinavia
clean_review_title: This is a very impressive book.
clean_review_text: The old stories and traditions have multiple layers of meaning. Varg looks at the information that is available about the cycle of the seasons and the cycle of human life and rebirth, and explores the Ragnarök story in that context. In doing so, he maps out a coherent, integrated model for the old religion. I am not sure that all of his premises and assumptions are completely accurate, but they work. "Accuracy" is perhaps a misleading concept anyway, because the practices of Eiríkr Þorvaldsson at Brattahlíð may have varied quite a bit from those of Hengest Wictgilsson, for example. What matters are the underlying values and beliefs. Varg has clearly done a lot of thinking about how the pieces fit together, and has produced a working model for the core mysteries. There are writers who go into specific aspects of the old religion in more depth and specificity, citing source material in a detailed way. Varg takes a different approach, and does the heavy thinking required to put it all together so that the reader can get a sense for how the religion might have functioned. Varg seems to spend a lot of time in nature, and appears to be strongly motivated by a sense of family and tradition. One gets the sense that by living on the margins of modern European culture, he is better able than most to conceptualize the spiritual needs of our forebears, and connect what is left of the lore to these needs. I guarantee that once you read this book, you will never think about the old religion in quite the same way again. A final note - I appreciated the rugged binding and paper of this paperback. This is a tough little book that one can stick in a jacket pocket, and pull out and read when the moment is right. An excellent formatting choice.

Not only is it tedious, you have to go through all kinds of extra steps that aren't part of the manufacturer's intended instructions to get it to make a decent cup of coffee. Plus, you gave to pack in a LOT of coffee to get a reasonable cup of coffee. Then, so it doesn't overflow and put grounds in your cup, I use a toothpick to kind of bore out the center so the water can flow in a little better. You can stop the leakage for the most part by putting a rubber band around the very top where the lid screws on, but if I'm paying as much as $20 for this, then I'd like it to work without my having to put in so much effort. I've also read several reviews about how quickly these break, and while mine has not yet broken, I can see where it will. Very shoddy construction. It's a disappointment. To sum it up, you CAN get a good cup of coffee out of this, but it's going to take work and practice to figure it out. product_title: Commercial Ice Maker Machine Self Clean, 100lbs/24H Stainless Steel Ice Machine 45 Cubes/Batch in 11-18 Minutes with 33lbs Ice Storage Bin, Freestanding Ice Maker for Restaurant/Home/Food Truck
clean_review_text: I've wanted a built-in ice maker for some time. There are 7 of us sharing the ice maker in a fridge and it just never cut it! I had two options in ice makers: nugget or cube. Nugget ice is much more fun, but it's not good for coolers. Cube ice works for both. So I got the cube maker. If I ever win the lottery, I'll get the other one too! Anyway, the machine was a pain to install, but that's not its fault. First I hired someone because they had to move my fridge, tap a water line, and break through the wall to install the drain. They screwed all of that up and I ended up with a leak, a damaged fridge, and the drain line was leaking as well. They fixed two of the three a week later and now it works well (though the fridge is another story). The instructions were poor, that caused some of the problem (even the professional misread them). I mean, if you're selling a $500 product, can't you afford proper instructions with real pictures written by someone who speaks English? All that aside, there is one thing I took off a star for: The unit doesn't stay frozen! It makes ice and dumps it in a fridge. It melts because that is a fridge not a freezer. And then it makes more ice. This causes a lot of wasted water and electricity, and it means that you absolutely must have that drain line installed! I had another one of these some time back that cost a lot less and made about half as much ice a day. That one was insulated and stayed below 0, so the ice never melted! I didn't even connect the drain line and in 4 years of use, it never leaked. If I were to go back to the start of this project, I would have gotten that again. That's the reason I took off a star. The other style would have saved energy and cost $200 instead of $500... But overall, considering that I got the item at a discount here, I'm okay with this outcome.

product_title: WonderWash Portable Washing Machine for Apartment & Tiny Spaces - Manual Hand Clothes Washer with Retro Design - Clean Laundry Anywhere with Our Countertop, Non-Electric, Small Washer - Blue
clean_review_title: A little messy, but works great!
clean_review_text: My washing machine broke at the start of the recent quarantine, and I'm still waiting for an appointment to have it fixed. With most laundromats closed, in desperation I bought a Wonderwash, hoping it would tide me over until I can get my washing machine fixed. This little machine is great! It is well-made and all the parts feel sturdy and high-quality. It arrives unassembled but is a breeze to put together, it takes five minutes and no tools. I highly recommend watching one of the instructional Youtube videos before doing your first load of wash, it explains the process thoroughly, but the basics are: determine load size, add a small amount of detergent and fill with hot or warm water to the fill line. Add clothes, tighten down the lid, then just turn the crank for two minutes or so (I time it by listening to a song on the radio.) The key is to turn the crank slowly, not quickly, and let the wet laundry fall from the top of the chamber to the bottom with a good thud each time. This drives the water and soap through the clothes and cleans them thoroughly. Once you're done, drain the chamber using the supplied pipe, then refill with cool water to the fill line and crank for two minutes again to rinse. My clothes came out really clean! Even garments with cooking stains washed right up. The only drawbacks are that water tends to splatter around a bit when you drain the chamber, and I found that to drain it completely when I was done I needed to turn the whole chamber upside down and let it set for a bit. I use the Wonderwash in my bathtub, so a little bit of water splashing around is not an issue. Other than that, this is a perfect little machine for doing small loads of laundry. It was a desperation buy, but I am really pleased with it! After my main washer is fixed, I intend to keep it to use in my RV camper. It would also be great for dorm or apartment dwellers.

product_title: GE PP9830DJBB Profile Series Electric Cooktop with 4 Burners and 3-Speed Downdraft Exhaust System, 30", Black
clean_review_text: This GE range was a replacement for a Jenn-Air CVE1400/4180/4210/4270 series downdraft for which we could no longer easily get parts. If you are considering the same swap, then you'll probably find challenges lining up with the existing duct-work. The required opening is the countertop is almost identical, the GE requiring 5/8" less distance from front to back and just 1/16" less side to side. So it slips into the old aperture easily, and with sufficient overlap that there are no gaps. The outlet to the ducting is 7/8" more to the right and 1-7/16" further to the back than the Jenn-air, which isn’t much but enough to cause problems because the fan box (plenum) on the GE is considerably longer, at a distance of 6-3/8" lower than the Jenn-Air. Added to that is the need for a 10" x 3-1/4" rectangular to 6" round transition adapter, which is longer than the required Jenn-Air 5" round to 6" round transition, all in all coming almost to the bottom of a standard cabinet, which leaves very little room to crank over toward the old duct-work. For me, the ducting was by far the most difficult and time consuming part of the install. When I later noted that the Amazon price for installation is $180, I thought that it was a great deal. Then looking at, "What's included with service" there is no mention of the duct-work. If you choose this option I advise checking with the installer. When installing the GE range you alternate between thinking, "this was thoughtful design" to, "why didn’t they just do this to make it easier". For example, when fitting the fan box you see an apparently useless hole in the top of the range cavity but you soon realize that it happens to line up with a dimple in the fan box that together mean that the weight of the box is supported while you install the fixing screws; good thinking. On the other hand, the first operation is placement of an adhesive foam gasket seal around the periphery of the underside of the glass top, and while doing this you wonder why the customer has to fit this, and not GE. The electrical box is attached with 2-screws, one at the back that is really hard to get to and one at the front for which your screwdriver will be difficult to access should your cabinet have a central divider. There seems no good reason why this box could not have been retained by screws to the sides. The instructions say that the electrical box takes a ¾" conduit, but mine had a hole for a 1" conduit, which could be a nuisance if you're not prepared. The GE range itself looks very sleek on the counter and so far has operated just as advertised. There is, however, room for improvement. There is only a single light to indicate that a heated element is on, but to see which requires checking the position of each control, which on our black control knob on black surface range, isn’t that easy. There are, however, "Hot Surface Indicator Lights", one for each side that remain on after switch-off as long as the glass on that side remains hot. The indicator to show if the front left control is on front-burner only, or front-burner and bridge, is a minute rectangle which is either filled or open, and is very hard to see. At the time of writing we are scared to death of spilling anything just in case it spoils the looks forever. I think that this range's surface is going to need a lot more cleaning than that of its predecessor, just like our granite countertops and stainless steel fridge. Progress? Form over function?

clean_review_title: Not my first Comfee’, but so far the best
clean_review_text: We had a smaller Comfee’ washing machine (.9 cub.ft) that worked great for 2 years. Then the little guy sprung a leak, it was out of warranty, and we decided to donate it and buy a bigger machine. This one IS bigger, fits a lot more. I have done about 6 loads so far, and if things change I will update the review. This time I bought an extended warranty for it, so it will be going back if something goes wrong. It is louder than my last one, you can hear the motor rev up when it does the spin cycles. In general this is better, it revs up…in increments, helping the load to balance in the bin before hitting top speed. Love this, versus my old machine that I would refill (a waste water) when it was out of balance to re-distribute the clothes. It does mean the motor can get a little loud when it does this, but not so much that you worry about neighbors hearing it or can’t have a phone conversation in front of it. Just enough for me to notice it was more than my last .9 cubic ft machine. One other thing, if you set it for 1 spin cycle - that doesn’t seem to include a full rinse (I expect it to fill the bin, move clothes around in fresh water, extract water and then spin). It seems to trickle water over the clothes as the bin moves around a few times and then it spins at high speed to do the final cycle. Sort of confusing, and I am still learning to see if there is another pre-set program that works differently, but I find choosing (2) spin cycles solves this issue. I expect a rinse cycle to do more, I guess! Otherwise it’s pretty good. I can do larger loads, with room so the clothes can move around and get clean. Will update if anything changes as I keep the machine longer.

product_title: 3406107 Dryer Door Switch by Beaquicy - Replacement for Whirlpool, Ken-More, Roper, Amana, Crosley Dryer (1-Pack)
clean_review_title: Easy Repair For A Very Common Issue
clean_review_text: If you're dryer fails to start or continues to run with the door open, then there's a very good chance that your dryer door switch has failed. More likely, the plastic tab that comes in contact with the door has broken off. This is a common problem that can be fixed by anyone in under 15 minutes. If you've never tackled this repair before, a quick YouTube search can show you how to access the lid switch for your dryer & then install your replacement. Just make sure to UNPLUG your dryer first!! I can't speak to the lifespan of this particular switch, but it's probably not as durable as the OEM part. However, the OEM part will be ridiculously overpriced for the same result. This particular part from Beaquicy is inexpensive enough that you can purchase two just to have a spare. And even then still not come near the cost of one OEM replacement. I've been running the dryer with the new switch in place since mid-Feb (2021) & I've had no problems. So, overall, I would consider this a successful repair & would have no issue recommending this part.

clean_review_title: No Trips to the Laundry Mat for me!!
clean_review_text: We are in the middle of a huge renovation...I currently have no kitchen or laundry room. I thought for sure I was going to have to go to a laundromat for several months, something I was dreading. Then I came to Amazon, and sure enough...someone had invented this neat little washer/dryer!! This little thing is amazing! I set it in my walk-in shower, plug it in, fill it up with dirty clothes (it holds more than you would think!), fill it up with water by using the shower hose (the hose that it came with didn't fit my faucet...but no big deal), pour laundry soap in. I wash for the full time (15 minutes). Drain...which is oddly satisfying to watch the dirty water come out. Then fill back up with water and fabric softener...run about a 3-minute cycle, then drain. You can't fit the same amount into the 'spinner', so it takes a couple of loads to spin the clothes. I only spin them for about 2 minutes, and they are surprisingly quite dry. Then I hang everything on a clothes dryer rack. I've been able to do a load of wash almost every evening, and it's working like a charm! I might just keep using this even when I finally get my new laundry room!

product_title: Whirlpool 786324 Igniter
clean_review_title: Worked miracles on an old Amana
clean_review_text: Our Amana AGS760WW range (from the early 80s) starting becoming slow to light and eventually just reeked of unlit gas. Some online research revealed that the igniter heats up by was of resistance and the flow of current then triggers the gas valve to open. A complete, but poorly performing ingniter would display my symptoms, so I sought out the right part. When shopping on some appliance part website, my handy Amazon Google Chrome plug in displayed a message saying that Amazon had it, and sure enough they did (and at 40% of the price) and it was available with Prime shipping! I never knew to look at Amazon. The first igniter arrived on a sub zero day, well packed but was broke. Having read other reviews I understood these to be highly brittle, and sure enough it was. As noted it was very well packed, but the conditions were less than ideal. A prompt replacement was dispatched, and it arrived intact. I skooted the oven out, unplugged the power (left the gas line connected), and removed the door and racks. The metal guard that shrouds the ignited was held in place by three easy to remove sheet metal screws, and the I pulled the old igniter out. At first glance, it seems much bigger than what's in my oven, but much get's hidden behind the back wall. It's an identical part. I fished the wire nuts out via the smallish hole to unscrew the old unit. I replaced the plastic ones with new ceramic wire nuts (rate for high temps, but the old ones should work). Note these were not included with the replacement part, but likely weren't needed. Then I put everything back into place. The hardest part was removing the metal guard from the old igniter and installing on the new one, especially while being fearful of it's brittle nature. All in all, it took 20 minutes, including putting the door back on (which can be tricky). Everything worked great, and the oven heats much faster than I've ever seen. It must have been going bad these last few years. I'm so glad I fixed this myself.

clean_review_title: Love love love it! Clothes are cleaner than ever before, and NO SOAP!
clean_review_text: UPDATE A YEAR LATER: We are still totally happy with the pureWash. For really soiled items, like the napkins and placemats we use daily, I sometimes do a pre-wash with a tiny bit of soap, but otherwise never use any at all! If we use stain stick on items like shirt collars, I might do a pre-wash as well, but without adding soap, just to get a little more wash time. One time I noticed the clothes were not clean when I removed them from the washer, and then realized someone had unplugged the pureWash. So you can definitely tell the difference. Our washer has an "eco-cold" water setting that uses no hot water at all. Apparently, most washers' "cold" setting uses a little hot water to help dissolve the detergent. Anyway, the colder the water, the better it works. We couldn't be happier with it. I still don't understand why utility companies and appliance salespeople aren't pushing this item more forcefully. ORIGINAL REVIEW: We recently had to replace our washing machine, and our local store had brochures about this unit. I'm surprised they didn't even try to sell it to me - I was just intrigued by the brochure and asked them about it. They charged about 25% less than the lowest cost I've seen online. Even though I used to wash mostly with cold water, I wanted it anyway because of its other features. First, it's just a cool gadget. Second, did I say this before? NO SOAP! Third, I no longer need to use hot water at all, even on towels and cloth napkins and cleaning rags like I used to. If you do use hot water, this should be what they call a no-brainer purchase for you. I predict this will be the next big thing in the home appliance industry, as long as the soap industry doesn't find a way to scuttle it. Between this and our new washer, which uses much less water than our old first-generation Neptune front loader, our water and electricity bills are definitely going to go down this year. Plus our clothes look cleaner than ever before. Whites actually look whiter, just like the old detergent ads used to say. This item is definitely a winner!

Wanted a nugget ice maker for our new wet bar and was all set to buy the GE Opal but was concerned about all the lukewarm reviews given its hefty price tag. After reading reviews for every competitive offering on Amazon decided to get the Crownful and so glad I did. Works great - love the easy access tilt up door and removable basket. Also love that you pour additional water right into same compartment instead of messing with removable tank (like our keurig has). If you zoom in on the photos and compare to the Opal they make identical ice. And this thing makes LOTS of it. Started producing in about 20 minutes and even with me scooping it up as fast as it could make it to put in a couple of mixed drinks it filled its basket in no time and has had no trouble keeping up since. The ice storage must be well insulated because even overnight there’s still plenty of unmelted ice the next morning. I think what pushed me to pull the trigger on this one was several reviews from people who’d gone through 2 or 3 Opals in a very short period of time. I’m thinking you could buy 2 of these for what the Opal costs and literally have a spare. Oh and here’s what else my research revealed: this and the Opal are the only 2 nugget ice makers Amazon sells an extended warranty on but it was $90 on the Opal and $12 on this one - that’s a no brainer. But it gets better - if you register it with the manufacturer they upgrade the first year limited warranty to a one year replacement warranty. Buy this! You won’t be disappointed.

product_title: Mr. Freeze MIM-18SI Maxi-Matic Portable Automatic Ice Maker with Lid, Silver
clean_review_title: A little noisy, and the ice is a bit melty, but it does what it says it will
clean_review_text: It runs fairly quickly, making ice inside of five minutes indoors in 80 degree weather (no air conditioning in the room it is in, but no direct sunlight either). It runs reasonably quietly. It will make a brief noise similar to a computer fan running at times. It will make a clunking noise as the ice drops into the basket. It will make a very loud beeping noise when the ice basket is filled or the water supply is empty. I'd rather it simply light a light (it automatically stops anyway) and not be so disruptive. This isn't something you want to leave running overnight anywhere you can hear it (not that it would make sense to do so, since it would just be making the same ice over and over and it would make more sense just to put the basket load in the freezer or start again the next day) and it's a little annoying for a movie night. That's the main reason it's losing a star. It doesn't keep the ice frozen. That's not its intended function. Instead it makes ice and then drops them in a basket above the water reservoir. As the ice melts the water fills the reservoir, from which it is then pulled to make new ice. You can have your choice of small or large cubes. It makes the kind of ice that has a hole in the middle, and not very thick walls even when set to large and that this gives the ice a lot of surface area, which means it melts very quickly. I would prefer the ice be a bit more solid, but a friend of mine thinks it's just fine if the ice is a bit "melty" so it's a matter of individual taste. It comes with a tiny ice scoop that would fill about half of a lowball glass with ice on a single scoop. It's very cute and very convenient. It has two buttons, one for power, the other for choosing if you want small or large cubes. It's about as easy to operate as a device can be. Overall, I think it will be a convenient device for entertaining in the summer.

After adding hot water and waiting for the prescribed time period, I easily remove the bag and have a pot of tea with a minimal amount of residue in the brew. Because a filled bag will remain open, I've also created some gifts by attaching a cotton string and little hand decorated tag to the bags. For the unsealed bag, be careful that the longer end does not hang over the edge of a cup, mug or teapot too much or you'll run the risk of the hot water being absorbed all the way to the upper edge, saturating it and possibly dripping onto the table top. This happened to me once because I forgot to retrieve the bag and the teapot sat for about a half hour. The bags can also be used for mulling spices because they are rather large. I love them!

clean_review_title: Love everything about Nespresso except pod $..!!
clean_review_text: 01/23 update: trying a different brand of lids when I ran out… I came back SO FAST!! Others failed me miserably!!! TBH, I was surprised to see any had issue using these- as these have single-handedly saved my coffee budget. If you take a minute to do the steps- truly, it can work like a charm! Finally- A fix to the costly pod price for Nespresso coffee maker! Not only does the “have to use pod“ cost a pretty penny.. but, they limit the ability to try different options in your coffee maker.. and limit any ability to go green. That’s a lot of control for one tiny appliance to have!! (IMHO!) Most important thing to know- SAVE YOUR OLD NEPRESSO PODS.. you NEED these in order to use these lids!! Every pod is marked with a barcode -hence the reason no other pod will work! This item is just the sealable aluminum foil top that lets you cover over up a refilled Nespresso pod. So, you must hold onto used pods in order to use this option. TBH, this was something I didnt realize when ordering..!! After cleaning, rinsing and drying your used pods 100%- you refill with the coffee of choice. **OK, this is where the foil lids come in! Carefully place the sealable purchased lid atop of pod.. BE SURE NOT TO OBSCURE THE BAR CODE FOUND ON SIDE..!! Foil is a tad bigger in diameter then pod cup itself- so it gives a bit of forgiveness when putting it on! DONT BE INTIMIDATED- I actually found prepping a handful at one time helps to find a rhythm and make things less of hassle vs. filling x1 every morning!! Coffee grounds stay fresh inside the sealed pod. Once shut these will store in whatever method you usually use- box, basket, display rack etc. all with the same fit.. the cute outer prints are a fun aesthetic! But, these designs are actually a way to differentiate between what you chose to fill the pod with. For example if using cappuccino = sunburst, decaf = stripes, etc. you have total of 4 designs. Hack: setting a roll of T.P. tipped tube side up- is a solid way to balance pod while adhering the new lid.. I know, sounds crazy but it steadies it for you!! See photo to show what I mean- Sorry so long winded- wanted to help others avoid any confusion. A great option for variety while saving both money and planet! Hoping info can help your decision.. stay safe everyone.

product_title: ZLINE 48 in. Convertible Vent Wall Mount Range Hood in Stainless Steel (KB-48)
clean_review_text: 5 stars for the initial review. Longevity is important for appliances...so we'll see how she does over the next months/years. Overall, I'm extremely pleased with this hood. For $300, it is what it is. It's eye-candy. Looks incredible and makes a huge statement. But everything else about it is just kind of "chincy." Not disappointed, as you have to expect that at this price point. The buttons don't push down very easily/evenly, the wires are dangling loose in the inner fan (some of their 3M tape came loose), some of the baffles fit loosely thus creating a lopsided effect (a gentle bending corrected the situation) the supplied screws/anchors are a joke. But hey, it looks incredible, and it does it's job...suck's the hot air. And for that, I'm stoked to spend only $300 on this! I've posted pictures for you to see....again, it looks awesome. The packaging job was incredible. And it comes coated with a protective film that protects the stainless finish until the final reveal. It is a little hard to peel off the film on the chimney extension unit. The other reviews said it scratches very easily...so I installed the chimney unit first, then peeled off the coating. I had to use a razor blade to score it just below the sight-line on one of the chimney extensions for a "clean break." Was a little nervous removing it from the box because some of the protective film had its own scratches. I was worried those would transfer to the stainless steel. Thankfully, they did not. The noise is a non-issue. It's a fan people...it's going to make noise. On the highest setting, you need to raise your voice if you're standing directly by the hood trying to have a conversation. But the other settings did not require my wife and I to "raise our voices." It's fine. Yes, all of the other reviewers are correct: buy new ductwork. The supplied plastic ductwork is a joke. It really is "easy" to install in that all you have to do is hang the thing. Everything else is literally plug and play! One more thought. We have a 30" stove and I naturally was going to buy the 30" hood. But right before my purchase, I started doing a little more research and many suggested to purchase a hood larger than your stove, to ensure it covers all of your burners. So at the last second, I opted for the 36" hood. SOOOOO glad I did. Not only does it look better on the wall, it efficiently "sucks" regardless of what is cooking on any of the 4 burners. Buy it. It's worth it. Let's just see how long it lasts......I'm cautiously optimistic. UPDATE 4/25/2013: After only our second time cleaning the stainless steel baffle filters, the screw/nut that holds the baffle together stripped, thus rendering it useless. Obviously, we were pretty disappointed. I sent an email to customer service asking what the next step of action should be. They responded that same day with a tracking number and I had a new baffle in 2 days with no cost. I've never experienced customer service like that. Really, just outstanding. I'm still worried that issues like this may arise again, but for the price-point, I have to expect that (one of the same screws on another baffle sits crooked in the baffle, causing it to align poorly). My wife and I still love this hood...and after this customer service interaction, we're only more pleased with the purchase!

clean_review_text: After five years seeing code E02 coming up, but washer still works fine. Cleaned out drain pump compartment, easy access, no tools required, just a rag and old toothbrush. Pros: highly efficient, good clean, far fewer problems with the septic system since going to a front loader (family of 7, lots of water use.) Cons: 1) Very low, need to raise it up. Helps to wipe door gasket out occasionally to prevent mold and grime buildup, not a big deal. Cycle time can be unpredictable, but supposedly due to sensors and machine intelligence. Normal cycle time starts out at 1:00, gets down to :50, then changes to 1:10. Overall been a good machine. Switched from decades of loyalty to Sears/Whirlpool after getting really screwed by Sears. Bought at Lowes, they delivered, installed, and took away the junk from Sears at no extra cost. The 300 doesn't have the frills of more expensive machines, but has all the features you need to get your clothes clean. "Kids Clean" cycle uses 150 degree water for tough jobs and sanitation. product_title: Whirlpool W10245259 Terminal Block, Black
clean_review_title: Perfect fit like an OEM replacement
clean_review_text: My Whirlpool range was only a few months old, but our oven stopped heating up right. Then stopped altogether. The stovetop worked fine, as well as the display. I contacted the manufacturer, and they kept asking for model numbers, purchase dates, serial numbers, "did you try this" - "did you try that"..... I'm no stranger to DYI and am very comfortable with electronics and electricity in general. WITH THAT SAID - if you are not comfortable or 100% sure you know what you're doing.... do NOT try to fix this yourself! With that spoiler alert said - Your stove works on 220V - 110v is for the stovetop and 110v for the broiler/oven. Since the top half worked, I knew it had to be the other side of the 220V. I pulled the oven out of the counterspace, and removed the protective cover for where this part is and found that on of the lugs was not put on correctly at manufacture. I knew it wasn't when the plug was put on, because that part was light, it was the inside lug that the lock nut was not tightened. For that reason, it was working for awhile, then wasn't making contact, and worked until it got hot, then it separated some, and stopped altogether when the wire burned off. Again, in my case, the plug from the range to the receptacle was fine, it was the wire from the lug to the oven that burned off. There is only one screw that holds this in place, but you need to be sure that you match the wires from your range to this lug panel, and you have to be sure to put your plug wires in the same place they were in OR you'll have a big problem. Make sure no wires are loose, and everything is snug. That's it. Any competent person can change this, but don't try this unless you have your electric off and you are confident in you ability. Anyway, this little part got my $900 range going again, and I didn't have to fight Whirlpool about whether the big box store I bought it from was responsible or them. The money this cost was worth it getting back to a fully functional appliance again.

product_title: Inkbird Temperature and Humidity Controller ITC-608T Pre-Wired Dual Stage Thermostat 120VAC 15A 1800W ETL Listed Mushroom Greenhouse Homebrew
clean_review_text: I’m in Alaska and temps have been -17 F every day for 2 weeks, and it’s only mid-November. With 2 1/2 - 3 months of temperatures like this, my chicken would have serious frostbite and maybe even die, without some heat even in their insulated coop. We put a radiant oil heater in the coop and use this Inkbird temperature controller to keep the heat at 34 degrees at roost level. The controller turns the electricity that runs the heater on at 33 degrees, and off at 35. The coop temp has been very consistent and gives me peace of mind. The floor of the coop is colder so the chickens are still acclimated to below freezing temps, but they get to rest and sleep above freezing which puts less stress in them. The set up was a little confusing, it helped to read the reviews here to understand what PV, SV, etc meant. Basically, set the temp for the highest you want it to go which is where you will want the heat to turn off. Then set the differential to the number of degrees you want the heat to kick on: I set mine at 35 a the max heat I want and where the electricity will kick off. I set the differential at 2, which is 2 degrees less than my max heat of 35. This means the electric will turn on when the coop is at at 33 degrees, and will cut off when the coop temp reaches 35 degrees. I am very happy with this unit.

product_title: 3 Pack: Mobii Custom Color, Small: Stim Restless Hand Fidget, Stress Ball Office School Toy
clean_review_title: Great colors! Perfect size! Nice movement!
clean_review_text: To begin, I have purchased each of the following (many in multiples): Möbii Daydream, Deep Thought, Half Thought, Medium Möbii, Medium Stainless Steel Möbii, Small Möbii, Small Stainless Steel and Copper Möbii, Anodized Aluminum Spin Ring, Stainless Steel Spin Ring. (All except the Small Stainless Steel/Copper Möbii and the Medium Stainless Steel Möbii are pictured above basically in the order they are listed. The two not pictured are the same size as the other Small and Medium Möbii and are well represented with the seller’s pictures. And, please ignore the busted knuckle from yard work. :) ) If you are like me, and like to comparison shop using reviews, I hope the inclusion of each item is helpful. That said, for simplicity, I will be copying and pasting the same review on each of the items I have purchased. While the Daydream is a bit large for my hand, it works because it is so lightweight. The individual links/rings are thicker than the Medium and Small Möbii, and I like the feel and movement. The Deep Thought is nice and heavy with a good feel to it. I purchased it as a gift to my husband. It fits well in his large hands, and he seems to like it. The Half Thought is wonderful! It is definitely one of my favorites. It has wonderfully simple and smooth unending movement. The weight is just right. It does make more noise than the tighter fitting Möbii fidgets—I mean, they’re stainless steel rings. The noise doesn’t bother me, and it’s basically as loud as you make it. The Small Möbii fidgets are really nice for discreet fidgeting at meetings, etc., and they work well for just fingertip fidgeting. The colors are vibrant, and the customization option is a lot of fun! The Small Stainless Steel and Copper Möbii is really nice—another favorite! The weight is great, and the movement is very smooth. I really like the copper mixed with the stainless steel. The Medium Möbii fidgets probably get the most use (yes, another favorite) because of the perfect size, nice movement, and fun colors. They are lightweight and quiet (not silent). I do also have a Medium Stainless Steel Möbii, which, of course, has a little more weight to it and a good feel. The Stainless Steel version probably has slightly better overall movement. Although, I do still tend to pick up the brightly colored Aluminum version more often. The Spin Rings (Anodized Aluminum and Stainless Steel) are both wonderful, whether you wear them at the base of the finger or in the middle—depending on fidget preferences. Of course, the Stainless Steel version is heavier. I really like both. I like more rings per spin ring for fidgeting and spinning, but obviously, more rings make the entire ring much thicker. If I wear a thick ring on my right (dominant) hand, it tends to interfere with writing. I usually just switch hands or set it aside. Everything I’ve received has been very well made and delivered in a cute little Möbii-branded bag. As other reviewers have stated, the personal, hand-written “thank you” note is a nice touch. I had a few large orders, and the seller even included a free Small Möbii in two of the shipments!! I was super excited! (Geoff, I hope you’re reading this: Thank you so much!!!) These make great gifts and have been a hit with my family and friends. I am addicted! I highly recommend any of these Steel Lynx products, y’all!! You can’t go wrong!

clean_review_title: Surprised by the large white border but otherwise excellent.
clean_review_text: It was unclear to me that there is a thick white border around the map composite. It says in the description that a border has been added to enhance the image, but honestly I thought that was referring to the small black border, which I knew I could hide under a frame. The white border is lost in the seller's photos and I wrongly thought that the title and description above and below were something that was added as a photo caption, and not on the composite itself. I guess it's just a matter of preference of whether you are hoping your finished piece looks like an actual framed map (that's what I was going for) in which case the map would go edge to edge or whether you want your finished pieced to look like a map composite reproduction with a caption describing what you're seeing (less interesting to me, visually). Perhaps the seller could photograph the item on a black background, or show it in a frame to highlight the presence of the white border? Or better yet, offer an option for having the map printed edge to edge with no border or caption? I realize I could crop the print myself however I've already ordered a frame made to the original dimensions. That said, the quality is superb, the shipping was lightning fast and the seller answered a question I had almost immediately. And I like the vision of what this seller is doing with old maps, thus the 5 stars!

clean_review_title: Truly sustainable w/ vegan formula; the clove is an interesting flavor/ scent
clean_review_text: J&L Naturals Sustainable Lip Balm is a truly sustainable product; from the stickers to the packaging, every piece is recyclable or biodegradable. The lip balms arrive in a cardboard box sealed with a low-adhesive sticker. Opening the package presents you with another sticker on the lid letting you know that your purchase planted a tree through #TEAMTREES; a QR code and web address provide you with a link to more info. The box is filled with Kraft paper crinkles to protect the lip balms in transit. The Lemongrass and Clove scent/ flavor was a pleasant surprise and a change from the usual flavors/ scents. The tubes of lip balm are where sustainability genuinely shines; the cardboard tubes push up rather than twist, and the balm itself is wider for better coverage. This formula is created from six safe, simple vegan ingredients: Organic Coconut Oil, Extra Virgin Olive Oil, Shea Butter, Candelilla Wax, Lemongrass Essential Oil, and Clove Essential Oil. There are six scents available, and one of them is unscented if you have sensitivity. The Bottom Line: At $27.49, each J&L Naturals sustainable tube is ~$5.50 each and holds more than twice as much as a standard plastic tube, which is excellent news for your bottom line. The vegan plant-based ingredients and plastic-free packaging are a boon for the planet. J&L Naturals Sustainable Lip Balm is a win-win product all around and would make a great stocking stuffer.

product_title: Ohana Means Family NuGold Necklace
clean_review_title: This is absolutely perfect! - 5 stars for the product plus bonus points for customer service
clean_review_text: When I saw this piece of handmade jewelry, I knew it was the perfect gift for my son's newly engaged wife to be for her birthday. And I can't say enough about Kiara, the lady who makes it. I had questions about delivery because it was a late find, and she was extremely prompt with her replies and also went out of her way to make sure to ship it early so that I got it in time. Such a sweet lady, and I am additionally proud to help support a military family. I hope she sells a lot of these and is very successful. Nearly 4 years ago, my son lost his young bride in a car accident, leaving him and their two kids without a wife and mother. Family has always been very important to us, but families huddle and take care of each other during those really rough times and we huddled a lot. Having to care for his family as well as support from his extended family and the family of his wife were the things that held him together. Just a few weeks ago, he became engaged again, planning to marry this June. Oddly, both his late wife and new fiance are the eldest of 5 siblings in very strong families, and we knew the minute we met her that she would become one of ours. And though they didn't realize the significance of it till a day later, my son proposed to her at the Ohana Restaurant at Disney World. How fitting is that?!? So when I saw this pendant, I knew it was the perfect gift for our future daughter in law. It's not expensive, but it is nicely made and simple elegance. Actually, the picture doesn't even come close to doing it justice, as it is so much nicer than expected. The seller puts the item in a small gift bag with a little bow on this, and I know our future daughter in law will be as tickled to receive it as I was to find it. Thanks Kiara for your prompt attention and assistance, and thank you to you and your husband for his service to our country.

product_title: Jigsaw Health Magsrt Magnesium (Pack of 2)
clean_review_text: MagSRT provides a higher dose than other available slow release formulas, and avoids the digestive distress that can be associated with regular release versions. However, given the cost, I would not recommend this as a first line magnesium supplement - try a regular release version, then a lower dose slow release if needed, and use this only if you require both the slow release and the higher dose. This version of MagSrt also provides B vitamin supplementation (B6, B12, Folate), which they say enhances absorption of the magnesium. The evidence for this is quite thin - some limited in vitro studies, but no in vivo human trials with positive results that I'm aware of. That said, there's no particular problem with the B vitamins being in the mix, except you may be taking something you don't need. Jigsaw does offer a B vitamin free version (MagSRT B Free), although the cost is the same. The pills are large, but not absurd (as some supplements can be), and have a uniquely smooth coating that makes them easy to get down. They have no particular odor or taste and no aftertaste. They can be split cleanly with a pill cutter, although I don't know what impact this has on absorption. Four stars for quality, one off because of the cost. A years worth, at the recommended dose (4 pills/day) is over $250.
